summary_one_line: Input needs a valid GSUB/GPOS table-directory entry whose magic 4CC tag and version-1 lookupList drive get_lookup_count past the default:return 0 arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed sfnt table directory carrying the literal 4-byte tag 'GPOS' or 'GSUB' at the record's tag field (winner bytes show 'GPOS'/'GSUB' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f) plus a version field of 0x0001 so that get_lookup_count's switch on u.version.major hits 'case 1' and returns (this+u.version1.lookupList).len instead of falling through to 'default: return 0;'.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry the exact ASCII magic tags 'GPOS'/'GSUB' concentrated at offsets 0x0c-0x0f and a 00 01 version prefix at offsets 0x00-0x05, so the version major resolves to 1 and the case-1 arm executes; the branch is taken 13370/10074 times and resolved in 10/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds have no concentrated 'GPOS'/'GSUB' tag at 0x0c-0x0f (scattered low bytes / non-tag values) and no valid version structure, so the version major is neither 1 nor 2 and execution lands on 'default: return 0;' (true side), keeping the false branch blocked in all 10 trials with hitcount 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operands from the 4-byte tag comparison and the version compare, then substitutes the exact 'GPOS'/'GSUB' / version-1 literals into the input at the matching offsets; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and must hit these multi-byte magic literals by chance, which they never do in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on the table version major in get_lookup_count, and the false side (non-default) is globally blocked.
Taking the winning side requires the case-1 arm dispatched by the version switch, returning the version1 lookupList length.
The case-1 arm is the winner-resolving path while the default arm is the blocker.
Winner seeds concentrate the literal GPOS/GSUB magic tags at offsets 0x0c-0x0f whereas loser seeds do not.
The fourth tag byte differs between winners (S/B for GPOS/GSUB) and losers, confirming the tag-literal requirement.
A winner seed literally contains the GPOS table tag at offset 0x0c.
A loser seed is a 6-byte fragment with no table tag, structurally incapable of presenting a GPOS/GSUB version-1 directory.
The winner reaches the branch thousands of times while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=13370  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one intercepted CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, explaining why the vpc>vp pair is also an I2S delta.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5550 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 002c9ad92acce91b`; the 31-level ancestor chain contains 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 29 and 30, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating get_lookup_count on a version-1 GPOS/GSUB directory where naive/value_profile resolve the false branch as often as cmplog/vpc within the budget, showing the multi-byte tag+version literal is not what I2S substitution uniquely supplies.

weakest_evidence_point: The version-1 (case 1) dependency is inferred from the switch source and the 00 01 prefix in winner seeds; the byte diff does not isolate the version field as a concentrated DIFFER offset the way the 0x0c-0x0f tag does.
